message.content:
Explore the skissify codebase to find:
1. Any existing NextAuth configuration (auth.ts, [...nextauth], etc.)
2. Any existing webhook endpoints (especially GitHub webhooks for auto-deploy)
3. The prisma schema
4. Any existing API routes under src/app/api/

Be thorough - check src/app/, src/lib/, src/auth*, etc.

content.text: Excellent! Now let me read the key files:

content.content:
1→generator client {
     2→  provider = "prisma-client"
     3→  output   = "../src/generated/prisma"
     4→}
     5→
     6→datasource db {
     7→  provider = "sqlite"
     8→}
     9→
    10→model User {
    11→  id            String    @id @default(cuid())
    12→  name          String?
    13→  email         String?   @unique
    14→  emailVerified DateTime?
    15→  image         String?
    16→  stripeCustomerId    String?   @unique
    17→  stripePriceId       String?
    18→  stripeSubscriptionId String?  @unique
    19→  stripeCurrentPeriodEnd DateTime?
    20→  accounts      Account[]
    21→  sessions      Session[]
    22→  sketches      Sketch[]
    23→  createdAt     DateTime  @default(now())
    24→  updatedAt     DateTime  @updatedAt
    25→}
    26→
    27→model Account {
    28→  id                String  @id @default(cuid())
    29→  userId            String
    30→  type              String
    31→  provider          String
    32→  providerAccountId String
    33→  refresh_token     String?
    34→  access_token      String?
    35→  expires_at        Int?
    36→  token_type        String?
    37→  scope             String?
    38→  id_token          String?
    39→  session_state     String?
    40→  user              User    @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    41→
    42→  @@unique([provider, providerAccountId])
    43→}
    44→
    45→model Session {
    46→  id           String   @id @default(cuid())
    47→  sessionToken String   @unique
    48→  userId       String
    49→  expires      DateTime
    50→  user         User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    51→}
    52→
    53→model VerificationToken {
    54→  identifier String
    55→  token      String   @unique
    56→  expires    DateTime
    57→
    58→  @@unique([identifier, token])
    59→}
    60→
    61→model Sketch {
    62→  id         String         @id @default(cuid())
    63→  slug       String         @unique
    64→  title      String         @default("Untitled")
    65→  data       String         // JSON string of SketchData
    66→  public     Boolean        @default(true)
    67→  userId     String?
    68→  user       User?          @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: SetNull)
    69→  sharedWith SharedAccess[]
    70→  createdAt  DateTime       @default(now())
    71→  updatedAt  DateTime       @updatedAt
    72→}
    73→
    74→model SharedAccess {
    75→  id       String  @id @default(cuid())
    76→  sketchId String
    77→  sketch   Sketch  @relation(fields: [sketchId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
    78→  email    String
    79→  canEdit  Boolean @default(false)
    80→
    81→  @@unique([sketchId, email])
    82→}
    83→

content.text: Now let me check for webhook endpoints:

content.content:
1→import { NextResponse } from "next/server";
     2→
     3→const manifest = {
     4→  name: "Skissify",
     5→  version: "1.0.0",
     6→  description: "AI-agent-compatible hand-drawn sketch tool. Define sketches as JSON and get beautiful hand-drawn style SVG/PNG output with organic wobble effects.",
     7→  url: "https://skissify.com",
     8→  api: {
     9→    createSketch: {
    10→      method: "POST",
    11→      path: "/api/sketches",
    12→      description: "Save a sketch and get a shareable URL. Body: { title?: string, data: SketchData, public?: boolean }",
    13→      returns: "{ slug: string, id: string }",
    14→    },
    15→    getSketch: {
    16→      method: "GET",
    17→      path: "/api/sketches/:slug",
    18→      description: "Retrieve a saved sketch by its slug",
    19→      returns: "{ data: SketchData, title: string, slug: string }",
    20→    },
    21→    manifest: {
    22→      method: "GET",
    23→      path: "/api/manifest",
    24→      description: "This endpoint. Returns the full schema and examples.",
    25→    },
    26→  },
    27→  schema: {
    28→    paper: {
    29→      type: "string",
    30→      enum: ["cream", "white", "yellow", "blueprint"],
    31→      default: "cream",
    32→      description: "Background paper style. 'cream' is warm beige, 'blueprint' is dark blue with light lines.",
    33→    },
    34→    tool: {
    35→      type: "string",
    36→      enum: ["pencil", "ballpoint", "ink"],
    37→      default: "ballpoint",
    38→      description: "Drawing tool. Pencil is thin/light, ballpoint is medium, ink is thick/bold.",
    39→    },
    40→    inkColor: {
    41→      type: "string",
    42→      default: "#222",
    43→      description: "Default ink color as hex. Elements can override with their own 'color' prop.",
    44→    },
    45→    wobble: {
    46→      amplitude: { type: "number", min: 0, max: 1, default: 0.7, description: "How much lines deviate from straight. 0 = perfectly straight, 1 = very wobbly." },
    47→      waves: { type: "number", min: 0, max: 1, default: 0.8, description: "Frequency of wobble waves. Higher = more wiggles per line." },
    48→      humanness: { type: "number", min: 0, max: 1, default: 0.15, description: "Random variation in stroke endpoints. Simulates imprecise hand placement." },
    49→    },
    50→    width: { type: "number", default: 1000, description: "Canvas width in pixels" },
    51→    height: { type: "number", default: 750, description: "Canvas height in pixels" },
    52→    elementTypes: {
    53→      line: { props: ["x1", "y1", "x2", "y2", "color", "strokeWidth"], description: "A wobbly hand-drawn line between two points" },
    54→      rect: { props: ["x", "y", "w", "h", "color", "fill", "strokeWidth"], description: "A hand-drawn rectangle. 'fill' is optional fill color." },
    55→      circle: { props: ["cx", "cy", "r", "color", "fill"], description: "A hand-drawn circle centered at (cx, cy) with radius r" },
    56→      arc: { props: ["cx", "cy", "r", "startAngle", "endAngle", "color"], description: "A hand-drawn arc. Angles in degrees." },
    57→      arrow: { props: ["x1", "y1", "x2", "y2", "color"], description: "Line with arrowhead pointing from (x1,y1) to (x2,y2)" },
    58→      text: { props: ["x", "y", "text", "fontSize", "color", "fontFamily"], description: "Hand-written style text. Uses Caveat font by default." },
    59→      dashed: { props: ["x1", "y1", "x2", "y2", "color", "dashLength"], description: "Dashed hand-drawn line" },
    60→      dim: { props: ["x1", "y1", "x2", "y2", "label", "color"], description: "Dimension line with measurement label and tick marks at endpoints" },
    61→      window: { props: ["x1", "y1", "x2", "y2", "color"], description: "Window symbol on wall -- perpendicular ticks at endpoints" },
    62→      "door-symbol": { props: ["x", "y", "w", "swing", "color"], description: "Door with arc showing swing direction. swing: 'left' | 'right'" },
    63→      "door-slide": { props: ["x", "y", "w", "color"], description: "Sliding door shown as parallel lines" },
    64→      stair: { props: ["x", "y", "w", "h", "steps", "color"], description: "Staircase with horizontal treads" },
    65→      opening: { props: ["x1", "y1", "x2", "y2", "color"], description: "Wall opening with small returns at each end" },
    66→      column: { props: ["cx", "cy", "size", "color", "shape"], description: "Structural column. shape: 'circle' (default) or 'square'" },
    67→    },
    68→    paperSizes: {
    69→      "A4+": { w: 1000, h: 750, description: "Default size, good for most sketches" },
    70→      A4: { w: 842, h: 595, description: "Standard A4 proportions" },
    71→      "A3+": { w: 1400, h: 1000, description: "Large format for detailed plans" },
    72→      "16:9": { w: 1280, h: 720, description: "Widescreen, good for presentations" },
    73→    },
    74→  },
    75→  examples: {
    76→    simpleBox: {
    77→      description: "A simple labeled box with an arrow -- minimal example",
    78→      data: {
    79→        paper: "cream",
    80→        tool: "ballpoint",
    81→        inkColor: "#222",
    82→        amplitude: 0.7,
    83→        waves: 0.8,
    84→        humanness: 0.15,
    85→        width: 540,
    86→        height: 420,
    87→        elements: [
    88→          { type: "rect", x: 100, y: 100, w: 200, h: 120 },
    89→          { type: "text", x: 150, y: 165, text: "Module A", fontSize: 18 },
    90→          { type: "arrow", x1: 300, y1: 160, x2: 420, y2: 160 },
    91→          { type: "rect", x: 420, y: 100, w: 200, h: 120 },
    92→          { type: "text", x: 475, y: 165, text: "Module B", fontSize: 18 },
    93→        ],
    94→      },
    95→    },
    96→    flowchart: {
    97→      description: "A simple decision flowchart with arrows",
    98→      data: {
    99→        paper: "white",
   100→        tool: "ink",
   101→        inkColor: "#1a1a1a",
   102→        amplitude: 0.5,
   103→        waves: 0.6,
   104→        humanness: 0.1,
   105→        width: 640,
   106→        height: 500,
   107→        elements: [
   108→          { type: "rect", x: 240, y: 20, w: 160, h: 60 },
   109→          { type: "text", x: 290, y: 55, text: "Start", fontSize: 20 },
   110→          { type: "arrow", x1: 320, y1: 80, x2: 320, y2: 130 },
   111→          { type: "rect", x: 220, y: 130, w: 200, h: 60 },
   112→          { type: "text", x: 260, y: 165, text: "Process Data", fontSize: 18 },
   113→          { type: "arrow", x1: 320, y1: 190, x2: 320, y2: 240 },
   114→          { type: "rect", x: 230, y: 240, w: 180, h: 70 },
   115→          { type: "text", x: 270, y: 280, text: "Decision?", fontSize: 18 },
   116→          { type: "arrow", x1: 320, y1: 310, x2: 320, y2: 370 },
   117→          { type: "text", x: 330, y: 345, text: "Yes", fontSize: 14, color: "#059669" },
   118→          { type: "rect", x: 250, y: 370, w: 140, h: 50 },
   119→          { type: "text", x: 290, y: 400, text: "Done", fontSize: 18 },
   120→          { type: "arrow", x1: 410, y1: 275, x2: 500, y2: 275 },
   121→          { type: "text", x: 440, y: 265, text: "No", fontSize: 14, color: "#dc2626" },
   122→          { type: "rect", x: 500, y: 250, w: 120, h: 50 },
   123→          { type: "text", x: 520, y: 280, text: "Retry", fontSize: 18 },
   124→        ],
   125→      },
   126→    },
   127→    floorPlan: {
   128→      description: "A small apartment floor plan with rooms, doors, windows, and dimensions",
   129→      data: {
   130→        paper: "cream",
   131→        tool: "ballpoint",
   132→        inkColor: "#333",
   133→        amplitude: 0.6,
   134→        waves: 0.7,
   135→        humanness: 0.12,
   136→        width: 800,
   137→        height: 600,
   138→        elements: [
   139→          // Outer walls
   140→          { type: "rect", x: 50, y: 50, w: 700, h: 500 },
   141→          // Room dividers
   142→          { type: "line", x1: 350, y1: 50, x2: 350, y2: 350 },
   143→          { type: "line", x1: 350, y1: 350, x2: 750, y2: 350 },
   144→          { type: "line", x1: 350, y1: 350, x2: 50, y2: 350 },
   145→          // Windows
   146→          { type: "window", x1: 150, y1: 50, x2: 280, y2: 50 },
   147→          { type: "window", x1: 450, y1: 50, x2: 600, y2: 50 },
   148→          { type: "window", x1: 50, y1: 400, x2: 50, y2: 500 },
   149→          // Doors
   150→          { type: "door-symbol", x: 350, y: 150, w: 60, swing: "right" },
   151→          { type: "door-symbol", x: 350, y: 420, w: 55, swing: "left" },
   152→          { type: "door-symbol", x: 200, y: 350, w: 55, swing: "right" },
   153→          // Entry door
   154→          { type: "door-symbol", x: 680, y: 550, w: 60, swing: "left" },
   155→          // Dimensions
   156→          { type: "dim", x1: 50, y1: 580, x2: 350, y2: 580, label: "6.0m" },
   157→          { type: "dim", x1: 350, y1: 580, x2: 750, y2: 580, label: "8.0m" },
   158→          // Room labels
   159→          { type: "text", x: 140, y: 200, text: "Bedroom", fontSize: 20 },
   160→          { type: "text", x: 480, y: 200, text: "Living Room", fontSize: 20 },
   161→          { type: "text", x: 140, y: 440, text: "Kitchen", fontSize: 20 },
   162→          { type: "text", x: 480, y: 440, text: "Bathroom", fontSize: 18 },
   163→          // Staircase
   164→          { type: "stair", x: 620, y: 370, w: 100, h: 150, steps: 8 },
   165→        ],
   166→      },
   167→    },
   168→  },
   169→  agentPrompt: `To create a Skissify sketch:
   170→
   171→1. Fetch https://skissify.com/api/manifest to learn the full schema
   172→2. Construct a JSON object with: paper, tool, inkColor, amplitude, waves, humanness, width, height, and an elements array
   173→3. Each element needs a "type" and type-specific properties (see elementTypes in schema)
   174→4. POST the JSON as { "data": <your sketch>, "title": "My Sketch" } to https://skissify.com/api/sketches
   175→5. You'll get back { "slug": "abc123" } -- the sketch is viewable at https://skissify.com/s/abc123
   176→
   177→Tips:
   178→- Use paper: "cream" + tool: "ballpoint" for the classic hand-drawn look
   179→- Keep amplitude 0.5-0.8 for natural wobble without being messy
   180→- For floor plans, use line/rect for walls, then add window/door-symbol/dim elements
   181→- For diagrams, use rect + text for boxes and arrow for connections
   182→- Text uses the Caveat handwriting font automatically`,
   183→};
   184→
   185→export async function GET() {
   186→  return NextResponse.json(manifest, {
   187→    headers: {
   188→      "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   189→      "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET",
   190→      "Cache-Control": "public, max-age=3600",
   191→    },
   192→  });
   193→}
   194→

content.text:
Perfect! Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Summary of Skissify Codebase Exploration

### 1. NextAuth Configuration

**File:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/auth.ts`

- **Auth Provider:** NextAuth v5 (beta.30)
- **Providers Configured:**
  - GitHub OAuth (requires `GITHUB_ID` and `GITHUB_SECRET`)
  - Google OAuth (requires `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID` and `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`)
- **Database Adapter:** PrismaAdapter from `@auth/prisma-adapter`
- **Custom Pages:** Sign-in page at `/auth/signin`
- **Session Callback:** Adds user ID to session object

**Route Handler:** `/w/code/skissify/src/app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts`
- Exports GET and POST handlers from the auth config

**Sign-in Page:** `/w/code/skissify/src/app/auth/signin/page.tsx`
- GitHub and Google OAuth buttons
- Client-side component with callback URL handling

---

### 2. Webhook Endpoints

**No GitHub webhooks found.** Only Stripe webhook exists:

**File:** `/w/code/skissify/src/app/api/stripe/webhook/route.ts`
- **Event Types Handled:**
  - `checkout.session.completed` - Updates user with Stripe subscription data
  - `invoice.payment_succeeded` - Updates subscription period end
  - `customer.subscription.deleted` - Clears subscription fields
- **Verification:** Uses `STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET` for signature validation

---

### 3. Prisma Schema

**File:** `/w/code/skissify/prisma/schema.prisma`

**Database:** SQLite with LibSQL adapter

**Models:**
1. **User** - Core user account
   - NextAuth fields: id, name, email, emailVerified, image
   - Stripe fields: stripeCustomerId, stripePriceId, stripeSubscriptionId, stripeCurrentPeriodEnd
   - Relations: accounts, sessions, sketches

2. **Account** - OAuth provider accounts (NextAuth standard)
   - Stores provider tokens and refresh tokens

3. **Session** - Active user sessions (NextAuth standard)

4. **VerificationToken** - Email verification tokens (NextAuth standard)

5. **Sketch** - User-created sketches
   - id, slug (unique), title, data (JSON string), public (boolean)
   - Soft ownership via userId (can be NULL for public sketches)
   - Relations: user, sharedWith

6. **SharedAccess** - Share permissions for sketches
   - email, canEdit (boolean permission)
   - Cascade delete on sketch deletion

---

### 4. API Routes

All located under `/w/code/skissify/src/app/api/`:

| Route | Method | Purpose | Auth Required |
|-------|--------|---------|---|
| `/auth/[...nextauth]` | GET, POST | NextAuth handler | No |
| `/manifest` | GET | API schema & docs | No |
| `/sketches` | POST | Create sketch | No (but rate-limited) |
| `/sketches` | GET | List user's sketches | Yes |
| `/sketches/[slug]` | GET | Load sketch | Conditional (private check) |
| `/sketches/[slug]` | PATCH | Update sketch | Yes |
| `/sketches/[slug]` | DELETE | Delete sketch | Yes |
| `/sketches/[slug]/share` | POST | Share sketch with email | Yes |
| `/stripe/checkout` | POST | Create checkout session | Yes |
| `/stripe/portal` | POST | Create billing portal session | Yes |
| `/stripe/webhook` | POST | Stripe events | No (signature-verified) |

**Rate Limiting:** In-memory rate limiter (60 requests/minute per IP) on sketch creation/updates

---

### 5. Supporting Libraries

- **Database:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/db.ts` - PrismaClient with LibSQL adapter
- **Stripe:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/stripe.ts` - Singleton Stripe instance, checkout & plan definitions
- **Validation:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/validations.ts` - Zod schemas for sketches with XSS sanitization
- **Plans:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/plan-check.ts` - Pro membership checks based on Stripe subscription
- **Rate Limit:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/rate-limit.ts` - In-memory sliding window rate limiter
- **Slug:** `/w/code/skissify/src/lib/slug.ts` - URL-safe slug generation

---

### Key Findings

- **No GitHub auto-deploy webhooks** - Only Stripe payment webhooks
- **Subscription model:** €2/month early adopter rate via Stripe
- **Feature gating:** Private sketches & sketch sharing locked behind Pro plan
- **Public sketches:** Free tier limited to 10, Pro unlimited
- **Data storage:** Sketches stored as JSON strings in SQLite
- **Auth flow:** NextAuth with GitHub/Google, sessions in DB
